_comment: Routines engine config -- schedule + task templates for recurring dispatcher intake.

_schema.routine_id: unique stable key -- used for backlog dedup and state tracking
_schema.task_template: merged with auto-fill defaults in backlog_append()

task_template.pipeline._purpose: When pipeline is set AND skills==[], dispatcher executes steps directly via subprocess (no claude -p, no worktree). ISC verification still runs against REPO_ROOT. Saves LLM tokens for routines that are pure script orchestration.
task_template.pipeline.steps: list of step dicts. Each step must have run_python (path under tools/scripts/), and may have args (list[str]), timeout_s (int <=1800), allow_nonzero (bool, default false)
task_template.pipeline.constraints: shell=False, list-form args only. No path traversal. Allowlist enforced by _validate_pipeline().

task_template.inline._purpose: When inline=true, dispatcher runs the worker on REPO_ROOT instead of an isolated worktree. Designed for producer routines that write to data/ outputs and don't need filesystem isolation. Refuses if REPO_ROOT working tree is dirty (cross-session safety). JARVIS_WORKTREE_ROOT is set to REPO_ROOT in the worker subprocess so the file containment guard still fires within the repo. Branch jarvis/auto-{id} is still created on REPO_ROOT.
task_template.inline.when_to_use: Producer-style routines that (a) write only to data/ or other gitignored / output-only paths, (b) don't need parallelism with other dispatcher tasks, (c) benefit from reading current REPO_ROOT state directly (no worktree clone lag).
task_template.inline.when_not_to_use: Routines that touch memory/, security/, CLAUDE.md, or any source-of-truth file -- those should keep worktree isolation.

task_template.description: Run /security-audit and produce structured report of findings
task_template.goal_context: Continuous security posture -- identify any new gaps since last audit
isc: A new security audit report exists in history/security/ dated today | Verify: find history/security -name '*audit*' -newer data/routine_state.json
No CRITICAL findings left unresolved | Verify: Review security audit output for CRITICAL severity items
task_template.notes: Routine-injected by routines engine. Tier 1 because /security-audit writes a dated report to history/security/; expected_outputs declares the write surface for scope_creep.

condition.description: skip synthesis if no signals queued

task_template.description: Run /synthesize-signals to distill accumulated learning signals into a synthesis doc
task_template.goal_context: Learning compounds -- threshold model is 10 signals / 72h. This routine fires every 3 days so the worst case is one missed cycle. If no signals are queued, /synthesize-signals exits Idle Is Success.
isc: A new synthesis document exists in memory/learning/synthesis/ dated today, OR worker reports Idle Is Success when no unprocessed signals queued | Verify: python tools/scripts/verify_synthesis_routine.py
Most recent synthesis doc cites >= 2 real source signals across >= 2 themes (anti-criterion: blocks hallucinated-themes fidelity failure) | Verify: python tools/scripts/verify_synthesis_recall.py
task_template.notes: Routine-injected by routines engine. Switched 2026-04-08: skill learning-capture -> synthesize-signals (skill mismatch -- learning-capture is for new signals, synthesize-signals is for distillation). interval 7 -> 3 to match the 72h tier in hook_session_start. routine_id kept stable for state continuity. Tier 1 + expected_outputs declares the write surface for scope_creep.

task_template.description: Run /update-steering-rules --audit to prune, merge, and categorize steering rules in CLAUDE.md
task_template.goal_context: CLAUDE.md steering rules decay -- stale rules waste context tokens every session; audit merges related rules and archives completed-phase references
isc: CLAUDE.md steering rule count is at or below 45 after audit | Verify: grep -c '^- ' CLAUDE.md
CLAUDE.md file size is at or below 20KB after audit | Verify: find . -name 'CLAUDE.md' -size -20k
All [MODEL-DEP] tagged rules reviewed against current Claude Code behavior -- remove if model limitation is fixed | Verify: grep -c MODEL-DEP CLAUDE.md returns 0 or each is confirmed still needed
A history/decisions/ entry documents the steering audit rationale | Verify: find history/decisions -name '*steering*' -newer data/routine_state.json
task_template.notes: Routine-injected by routines engine -- tier 1, human review recommended

task_template.description: Run prediction pipeline: (1) python tools/scripts/prediction_event_generator.py to generate+approve events, (2) python tools/scripts/prediction_backtest_producer.py to run backtests and post Slack review summary
task_template.goal_context: Autonomous prediction calibration -- generate domain-balanced pre-2022 events, run backtests, post Slack summary for Eric to review verdicts. Pipeline auto-approves events; human gate is at the review stage.
isc: At least 1 new backtest prediction file written to data/predictions/backtest/ | Verify: find data/predictions/backtest -name '*.md' -newer data/backtest_state.json
Slack summary posted with per-event verdict prompts | Verify: grep -c 'Backtest Review Summary' data/logs/prediction_backtest_*.log || echo check-slack
All events have knowledge_cutoff_date before 2022-01-01 (anti-criterion) | Verify: grep 'knowledge_cutoff_date' data/backtest_events.yaml | grep -v '201\|202[01]'
task_template.notes: Two-step pipeline: (1) prediction_event_generator.py generates + auto-approves events, (2) prediction_backtest_producer.py runs backtests + posts Slack review summary. Human reviews via Slack reply. inline=true: writes only to data/, no need for worktree isolation.

task_template.description: Run prediction backtest producer -- execute up to 3 unrun historical events from backtest_events.yaml
task_template.goal_context: Prediction calibration bootstrap -- run constrained historical predictions to build accuracy signal base
isc: At least 1 new backtest prediction file written to data/predictions/backtest/ | Verify: find data/predictions/backtest -name '*.md' -newer data/backtest_state.json
All output files carry backtested=true and leakage_risk=HIGH in frontmatter | Verify: grep -l 'backtested: true' data/predictions/backtest/*.md
backtest_state.json updated with run date for each executed event | Verify: Read data/backtest_state.json
task_template.notes: Runs prediction_backtest_producer.py -- outputs tagged pending_review, require Eric approval before calibration use. inline=true: writes only to data/.

task_template.description: Run prediction weekly review task -- surface due predictions and backtest reviews in Slack
task_template.goal_context: Close the prediction resolution loop -- Eric resolves via Slack reply to keep calibration data flowing
isc: Slack notification posted to #epdev listing all predictions due within 30 days | Verify: Check #epdev Slack for prediction_review message dated today
Log file written to data/logs/prediction_review_{date}.log | Verify: find data/logs -name 'prediction_review_*' -newer data/routine_state.json
No forward prediction open for >90 days without a resolution prompt | Verify: Read log file (anti-criterion)
task_template.notes: Runs prediction_review_task.py -- low-cost Slack surface task. inline=true: writes only to data/logs.

task_template.description: Run prediction calibration loop -- compute per-domain accuracy and bias adjustments
task_template.goal_context: Calibration feedback -- compute domain-specific accuracy and inject adjustments into /make-prediction
isc: data/calibration.json exists with per-domain adjustment values | Verify: Read data/calibration.json
data/calibration_narrative.md exists with prose bias summary | Verify: test -f data/calibration_narrative.md

Calibration adjustments are within [-0.15, +0.15] bounds | Verify: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('data/calibration.json')); assert all(-0.15<=v['adjustment']<=0.15 for v in d['domains'].values())"
No calibration signal written without >= 20 forward-looking resolved predictions | Verify: Check calibration.json threshold field (anti-criterion)
task_template.notes: Runs prediction_calibration.py -- checks threshold before executing; haiku for counting, sonnet for analysis. inline=true: writes only to data/.

task_template.description: Run branch lifecycle scan -- delete merged branches, flag stale ones for review
task_template.goal_context: Branch hygiene -- merged branches waste git namespace; stale branches indicate abandoned work
task_template._isc_followup_2026-04-08: Original ISCs used 'python ...' as verify, which is blocked by ISC_ALLOWED_COMMANDS (python removed for sandbox-escape risk). Both criteria were silently no-ops. Replaced with file-exists stub for pipeline prototype. Real fix: have branch_lifecycle.py write data/branch_lifecycle_state.json with merged_count and stale_count fields, then verify via 'find ... -newer' + grep.
isc: branch_lifecycle.py is present (prototype stub -- see _isc_followup) | Verify: test -f tools/scripts/branch_lifecycle.py
task_template.notes: Runs branch_lifecycle.py via deterministic pipeline -- no claude -p, no worktree, ~0 LLM tokens. Was haiku-wrapped script orchestration.

task_template.description: Doc-sync check -- verify tasklist checkboxes match actual artifacts, file paths are current, counts are accurate
task_template.goal_context: Tasklist is Eric's primary trust tool -- stale checkboxes erode trust and cause duplicate work
isc: No tasklist items marked [x] reference files that don't exist | Verify: grep -oP '`[^`]+`' orchestration/tasklist.md | head -20
Phase status line in tasklist matches actual completion state | Verify: Read orchestration/tasklist.md
task_template.notes: Read-only audit -- reports discrepancies, does not auto-fix

task_template.description: Run python tools/scripts/dream.py --autonomous to consolidate memory and merge cross-tier duplicates
task_template.goal_context: Memory hygiene -- merge duplicates, prune stale pointers, promote synthesis themes; cross-tier merge added 2026-04-07
isc: data/dream_last_report.md exists with today's date | Verify: Read data/dream_last_report.md
data/dream_last_run.txt mtime is within last 36h | Verify: stat data/dream_last_run.txt
Cross-tier merges (if any) wrote donor files to memory/learning/signals/pruned/ not unlinked | Verify: ls memory/learning/signals/pruned/
task_template.notes: Deterministic worker -- haiku for any LLM-touched decisions; mostly stdlib + embedding service. Tier 1 because dream.py writes report/log/index/pruned files; expected_outputs declares the write surface for scope_creep.

task_template.description: Backlog health review -- surface stuck tasks, stale pending_review items, and backlog composition stats
task_template.goal_context: Pipeline health depends on backlog flow -- stuck tasks and stale reviews block forward progress
isc: No pending_review tasks older than 7 days exist in backlog | Verify: python -c "import json,datetime; tasks=[json.loads(l) for l in open('orchestration/task_backlog.jsonl')]; print([t['id'] for t in tasks if t.get('status')=='pending_review'])"
Backlog composition stats posted to Slack (pending/done/failed counts) | Verify: check #epdev for backlog_health message
task_template.notes: Lightweight audit -- haiku can count and report; escalates stale items to Slack. inline=true: read-only audit, no source-of-truth writes.
